posts.author: AbhijeetSakhare
posts.community: Travel Food Services (TFS) – Riding the Premiumization & Infrastructure Wave
posts.text: My view: Agree. TFS has the operating edge and pricing power. Airport operators can take some share, but they cannot fully replace TFS. Good point. Udaan Cafes may impact low-ticket spending, but TFS is stronger in lounges and post-security outlets. Such outlets will likely remain important in the future. True, promoter overhang is there, but if execution stays strong, the story should hold. TFS still looks well placed. Airport owners can partner, but specialist execution is not easy to replicate. Disclosure: Invested, so my view may be biased.

posts.author: drjpatwa
posts.community: Travel Food Services (TFS) – Riding the Premiumization & Infrastructure Wave
posts.text: I initiated position in TFS last week around 1050 as price was good. Following are concerns as per me & counter arguments of it. Airport operator like Adani & GMR can snatch away TFS business. The argument seems to be true & is major threat. However TFS already have JV with Adani & GMR for business. So, What changes here - Earlier TFS was sole beneficiary of business but now Airport operators also wants take pie of it. Can Airport operators completely replace TFS? - I think no. Because TFS has deep expertise in food business which airport operator doesn’t have e.g. To manage lounge & food outl…
